site.name: Mei Kitchen
site.tagline: A reference kitchen across seven cuisines
site.description: A reference kitchen across seven cuisines — Chinese, Nyonya, Malay, Indian, Thai, Japanese, French. Trained by renowned chefs in Singapore, Australia, and Thailand. Trusted recipes, family-friendly, properly explained, for cooks at home and in the diaspora.

home.lede: Recipes from a working kitchen. Second-generation chef, trained at the elbow of a chef father and apprenticed under renowned chefs in Singapore, Australia, and Thailand. Every recipe here has been cooked many times, eaten by people who told the truth, and re-written until the proportions stopped being wrong.
home.signatureLede: The recipes that found their own audience — re-posted, requested, and remembered.
home.basicsLede: The building blocks — pastries, stocks, sauces, doughs. The recipes other recipes are built from.

familyKitchen.title: A family kitchen, not a stage
familyKitchen.body: Many recipes here are marked with steps a child can do safely — measuring, mixing, kneading, decorating. The book this collection began from is named for a question every cook of small children has heard. Toggle the family-friendly view to surface those steps clearly.

creds.title: About the kitchen
creds.bookTitle: Can I lick the spoon, mum?
creds.training: Trained by renowned chefs in Singapore, Australia, and Thailand.
creds.wok: Designer and maker of the Mei Wok.

home.watchLede: Three short pieces from the kitchen — wok work, weeknight prawns, and curry puffs improvised in a hotel room.
home.watchVideo3: Curry puffs in a hotel room

search.title: Search
search.noQuery: Search for a recipe, ingredient, or cuisine.

signature.title: The recipes that travelled
signature.lede: Recipes that found their own audience — re-posted across blogs, requested by readers, and remembered. Each one comes with its full story.

basics.title: The Basics
basics.lede: The building blocks of cooking — breads, cakes, fillings, noodles and pastas, pastry, and the stir-fry. Master a basic and a hundred recipes open up.
basics.variantsLede: Some basics have more than one legitimate form — Chinese and Western puff pastry are both correctly called puff pastry, but they are not interchangeable.
basics.usedInEmpty: No recipes have linked to this basic yet.

sectionsLede.breads: Doughs that rise, doughs that don't. The starting point for everything from sandwich loaves to bao.
sectionsLede.cakes: Creaming, whipping, one-bowl. The methods, then the variations.
sectionsLede.fillings: Custards, jams, savoury mixtures. What goes inside the things you bake.
sectionsLede.noodlesAndPastas: Wheat, rice, alkaline. Made from scratch and shaped by hand.
sectionsLede.pastry: Shortcrust, puff, choux. The geometry of butter and flour.
sectionsLede.stirFrySixSteps: Six steps that turn any combination of vegetables and protein into dinner. The most useful page on this site.

categories.title: Browse by category
categories.lede: Recipes across seven cuisines — Chinese, Nyonya, Malay, Indian, Thai, Japanese, and French — alongside method and meal categories. Pick a section to start.

book.title: Can I lick the spoon, mum?
book.fullTitle: Can I Lick the Spoon, Mum?
book.fullSubtitle: A Comics-Style Cookbook for Creating Asian Bakes and Family Memories in the Kitchen
book.chineseTitle: 妈妈,我可以舔舔勺子吗?
book.subtitle: Finalist, Singapore Book Awards 2025
book.byline: By Pamela Lim · Illustrated by Japher Lim and Eliz Ong
book.publisher: Published by WS Education, World Scientific Publishing — August 2024
book.ledeP1: A cookbook for parents and children to use together. Comics-style, easy to follow, built from 22 base recipes that open into 20 further variations.
book.ledeP2: From basic butter cookies to mocha, matcha, and peanut — and on to pineapple tarts and other Asian bakes. The recipes a family learns by cooking them together, again and again, until they belong to everyone in the kitchen.
book.originStory: Originally conceived as a project with my son Japher; when his time ran out, Eliz Ong stepped in to complete the illustrations.
book.specPublisher: WS Education (World Scientific Publishing Co Pte Ltd)
book.buyLine: Available from major Singapore bookshops, World Scientific, and online.
book.excerptHeading: From the kitchen, into the book
book.excerptLede: The book's 22 base recipes and their 20 variations live on this site too, marked "From the book" where they appear. Many recipes here did not make the edit — they are kept in the notebook regardless.
book.recipesLede: Recipes drawn from the book also live on this site, with the full method written out. The list grows as more book recipes are added to the working notebook.
book.recipesEmpty: Book recipes are being added to the site as Pamela cooks them. Check back soon.

press.title: Selected publications
press.lede: Magazine features, book reviews, and media appearances. Listed in reverse chronological order.
press.empty: Press list being compiled. Send the magazine titles, dates, and any cover images and they will appear here.

about.title: About this kitchen
about.intro: I am a second-generation cook. My father was a chef, and I learned at his elbow.
about.p1: I have trained under renowned chefs in Singapore, Australia, and Thailand — across cuisines that include Chinese, Nyonya, Malay, Indian, Thai, Japanese, and French. I also completed a patisserie certification at Le Cordon Bleu in Sydney; that was one chapter, not the foundation.
about.p2: This site is what a working kitchen notebook looks like written down. Every recipe here is something I would cook again. The order of operations matters; the order of words has been argued over. Ingredients are listed the way you reach for them, not the way they were bought.
about.p3: Measurements lean metric. Salt is to taste. Heat is honest. If a step says "do not stir," it means it.
about.p4: My book, Can I lick the spoon, mum?, was a <award>finalist for the Singapore Book Awards in 2025</award>. I have been featured in several food publications. I designed and manufactured the Mei Wok, used in this and most of the wok recipes here.
about.signoff: Cook from anything. Trust your hands.
about.portraitAlt: Pamela Lim — second-generation cook, author, wok-maker
about.kitchenAlt: The family kitchen where the recipes were tested
about.lcbAlt: In the Le Cordon Bleu Sydney patisserie class

wok.title: The Mei Wok
wok.subtitle: Designed and manufactured by Pamela Lim.
wok.lede: A wok built for a working kitchen — the one used in this site's wok recipes. Story, design notes, and where to find it.
wok.videoPending: Video coming. The page is here in the meantime.
wok.designLede: Notes on the shape, the steel, the handle, and the decisions that went into a wok meant for daily use rather than display.

footer.tag: Made with butter, salt, and patience.
footer.wok: The Mei Wok — designed and manufactured by Pamela Lim
